findings.message: New pydantic field `widget_timeout_s` is consumed under the `APP_` prefix (env var `APP_WIDGET_TIMEOUT_S`) but is not declared in `.env.example`. A consumed-but-undeclared variable breaks DEV->CI->STAGE->PROD env-var parity: operators have no documented surface to configure the widget call timeout, so it silently falls back to the hardcoded 5.0 default in every deployed environment.
findings.suggestion: Add `APP_WIDGET_TIMEOUT_S=5.0` (with a brief comment) to `.env.example` so the variable is declared on the environment surface alongside the other APP_* fields.

A consumed-but-undeclared variable breaks DEV->CI->STAGE->PROD env-var parity: operators have no documented surface to configure the widget call timeout, so it silently falls back to the hardcoded 5.0 default in every deployed environment.","suggestion":"Add `APP_WIDGET_TIMEOUT_S=5.0` (with a brief comment) to `.env.example` so the variable is declared on the environment surface alongside the other APP_* fields."}]
